eventMetadata.context_description: Luciano Acosta's elite form anchors trader sentiment for FC Cincinnati vs. CF Montréal player props, with the MVP candidate leading MLS in goals and assists amid Cincy’s eight-match unbeaten streak. Official injury reports confirm Acosta is fully fit, boosting overs on his shots and goal contributions against Montréal’s league-worst defense conceding 2.1 goals per game. Cincinnati’s home dominance (7-2-4 record) contrasts Montréal’s road woes (3-7-6), where key forward Romell Quioto remains sidelined by hamstring strain. Recent 3-0 Cincy romp over Miami highlights their attack, while Montréal’s three straight losses signal defensive fragility; rest advantage favors the hosts in this playoff-chasing Eastern Conference clash.

question: Kwadwo Opoku: Anytime Goalscorer
description: In the upcoming MLS game between FC Cincinnati and CF Montréal, scheduled for March 22, 2026 at 1:00 PM ET: This market will resolve to "Yes" if Kwadwo Opoku is credited with a goal in the official box score within the first 90 minutes of regular play plus stoppage time. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No". Own goals do not count. If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed. If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve "50-50". If the player is listed as inactive or otherwise does not play, the market will resolve "No". The primary resolution source for this market is the official statistics of the event as recognized by the governing body or event organizers. However, if the governing body or event organizers have not published final match statistics within 24 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

question: Will Iran strike Jordan again in March?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if Iran initiates a drone, missile, or air strike on the listed country's soil or any official embassy or consulate of the listed country between market creation and March 31, 2026, 11:59 PM (ET). Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

For the purposes of this market, a qualifying "strike" is defined as the use of aerial bombs, drones or missiles (including cruise or ballistic missiles) launched by Iranian military forces that impact a listed country's ground territory or any official embassy or consulate of that country (e.g., if a weapons depot on a listed country soil is hit by an Iranian missile, this market will resolve to "Yes").

Missiles or drones which are intercepted and surface-to-air missile strikes will not be sufficient for a "Yes" resolution regardless of whether they land on a listed country's territory or cause damage.

Strikes within the West Bank or the Gaza Strip, will be counted as Israel.

Only military actions by Iranian forces explicitly claimed by the Islamic Republic of Iran, or confirmed to have originated from Iranian territory, will count toward the resolution of this market. Attacks by proxy forces (i.e. Hezbollah, Houthis, etc.) will not count toward the resolution of this market.

Actions such as artillery fire, small arms fire, FPV or ATGM strikes directly, ground incursions, naval shelling, cyberattacks, or other operations conducted by US or Israeli ground operatives will not qualify.

The resolution source will be a consensus of credible reporting.

If the date/time of a strike cannot be confirmed by a consensus of credible reporting by the end of the third calendar date after this market's end date, it will resolve to "No" regardless of whether a strike was later confirmed to have taken place.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus implies low odds for Iranian military action against Israel by March 31, driven by demonstrated Israeli air defenses intercepting over 99% of Iran's April and October 2024 missile barrages, coupled with U.S. deterrence under the incoming Trump administration's hawkish posture. Recent developments include Israel's targeted killings of Hezbollah leader Hassan Nasrallah and IRGC commanders, prompting Tehran's restrained responses to avoid broader war, alongside a fragile Lebanon ceasefire in late November. Proxy escalations via Houthis and Hezbollah continue, but direct confrontation risks remain muted. Key upcoming events include IAEA nuclear inspections in Iran and potential U.S.-Israel joint exercises, which could either ease or heighten regional tensions.

For the purposes of this market, a qualifying "strike" is defined as the use of aerial bombs, drones or missiles (including cruise or ballistic missiles) launched by Iranian military forces that impact a listed country's ground territory or any official embassy or consulate of that country (e.g., if a weapons depot on a listed country soil is hit by an Iranian missile, this market will resolve to "Yes").

Missiles or drones which are intercepted and surface-to-air missile strikes will not be sufficient for a "Yes" resolution regardless of whether they land on a listed country's territory or cause damage.

Strikes within the West Bank or the Gaza Strip, will be counted as Israel.

Only military actions by Iranian forces explicitly claimed by the Islamic Republic of Iran, or confirmed to have originated from Iranian territory, will count toward the resolution of this market. Attacks by proxy forces (i.e. Hezbollah, Houthis, etc.) will not count toward the resolution of this market.

Actions such as artillery fire, small arms fire, FPV or ATGM strikes directly, ground incursions, naval shelling, cyberattacks, or other operations conducted by US or Israeli ground operatives will not qualify.

The resolution source will be a consensus of credible reporting.

If the date/time of a strike cannot be confirmed by a consensus of credible reporting by the end of the third calendar date after this market's end date, it will resolve to "No" regardless of whether a strike was later confirmed to have taken place.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus implies low odds for Iranian military action against Israel by March 31, driven by demonstrated Israeli air defenses intercepting over 99% of Iran's April and October 2024 missile barrages, coupled with U.S. deterrence under the incoming Trump administration's hawkish posture. Recent developments include Israel's targeted killings of Hezbollah leader Hassan Nasrallah and IRGC commanders, prompting Tehran's restrained responses to avoid broader war, alongside a fragile Lebanon ceasefire in late November. Proxy escalations via Houthis and Hezbollah continue, but direct confrontation risks remain muted. Key upcoming events include IAEA nuclear inspections in Iran and potential U.S.-Israel joint exercises, which could either ease or heighten regional tensions.

Actions such as artillery fire, small arms fire, FPV or ATGM strikes directly, ground incursions, naval shelling, cyberattacks, or other operations conducted by US or Israeli ground operatives will not qualify.

The resolution source will be a consensus of credible reporting.

If the date/time of a strike cannot be confirmed by a consensus of credible reporting by the end of the third calendar date after this market's end date, it will resolve to "No" regardless of whether a strike was later confirmed to have taken place.

events.title: Iran military action against ___ by March 31?
events.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if Iran initiates a drone, missile, or air strike on a listed country's soil or any official embassy or consulate of a listed country on the listed date (ET). Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

For the purposes of this market, a qualifying "strike" is defined as the use of aerial bombs, drones or missiles (including cruise or ballistic missiles) launched by Iranian military forces that impact a listed country's ground territory or any official embassy or consulate of that country (e.g., if a weapons depot on a listed country soil is hit by an Iranian missile, this market will resolve to "Yes").

Missiles or drones which are intercepted and surface-to-air missile strikes will not be sufficient for a "Yes" resolution regardless of whether they land on a listed country's territory or cause damage.

Strikes within the West Bank or the Gaza Strip, will not be counted as Israel.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus implies low odds for Iranian military action against Israel by March 31, driven by demonstrated Israeli air defenses intercepting over 99% of Iran's April and October 2024 missile barrages, coupled with U.S. deterrence under the incoming Trump administration's hawkish posture. Proxy escalations via Houthis and Hezbollah continue, but direct confrontation risks remain muted. Key upcoming events include IAEA nuclear inspections in Iran and potential U.S.-Israel joint exercises, which could either ease or heighten regional tensions.

question: Will the total number of TSA passengers for March 20 be less than 2,400,000?
description:
This market will resolve to the total number of TSA passengers reported on March 20, 2026. 

If the reported total number of TSA passengers falls exactly between two brackets, then this market will resolve to the higher range bracket.

This market will resolve as soon as throughput data becomes available for the listed date. Any revisions published to data for dates December 1, 2025 and onward prior to the release of data for all dates within the listed range will be considered.

If data is not available for the listed date by April 30, 2026, this market will resolve to "Other".

The resolution source for this market will be the daily checkpoint throughputs as measured by the US Transportation Security Administration (TSA), https://www.tsa.gov/travel/passenger-volumes. Should this URL change or move locations, a new URL on the tsa.gov domain will remain valid to resolve this market.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket heavily favors 2.8 million to 3.0 million TSA passengers screened on March 20, reflecting recent upward trends in daily traveler throughput amid lingering spring break demand. Official TSA data shows March 19 screenings at 2.55 million, up from 2.42 million the prior Tuesday, with weekend peaks exceeding 2.9 million earlier in the month. Weekday patterns typically range 2.4-2.6 million, but sustained post-holiday travel volumes and favorable weather have pushed recent figures higher, elevating probabilities for the upper bins while discounting sub-2.4 million outcomes. The next TSA report, expected March 21, will confirm the count.

This market will resolve to the total number of TSA passengers reported on March 21, 2026. 

If the reported total number of TSA passengers falls exactly between two brackets, then this market will resolve to the higher range bracket.

This market will resolve as soon as throughput data becomes available for the listed date. Any revisions published to data for dates December 1, 2025 and onward prior to the release of data for all dates within the listed range will be considered.

If data is not available for the listed date by April 30, 2026, this market will resolve to "Other".

The resolution source for this market will be the daily checkpoint throughputs as measured by the US Transportation Security Administration (TSA), https://www.tsa.gov/travel/passenger-volumes. Should this URL change or move locations, a new URL on the tsa.gov domain will remain valid to resolve this market.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus heavily favors 2.4 million to 2.6 million TSA checkpoint passengers on March 21, driven by sustained spring break travel demand evident in prior days' throughput—March 19 screened 2.35 million and March 20 hit 2.48 million, per official TSA data. This aligns with historical mid-March averages around 2.4 million amid school vacations, without major disruptions like widespread weather delays or airline issues reported. Lower odds for sub-2.4 million reflect steady recovery from winter lulls, while higher bins remain improbable given no surge catalysts like holidays. Upcoming Easter weekend could influence trends, but current pricing emphasizes recent upward momentum in daily screenings.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket heavily favors 2.7 million to 2.9 million TSA checkpoint passengers for March 22 at 67.5% implied probability, reflecting steady spring break travel momentum amid favorable weather and minimal disruptions. Recent daily throughput data shows March 20 at 2.65 million and March 21 at 2.82 million, aligning with seasonal upticks on Fridays during peak vacation periods, up from mid-February averages near 2.5 million. Lower buckets like under 2.5 million carry just 6% odds, as no major airline delays, FAA ground stops, or storms have materialized to suppress volumes, while higher ranges above 3.1 million remain marginal given historical precedents capping weekdays below Easter surges. Official TSA figures, typically released next day, will resolve the market.

question: Will "The Romantic" by Bruno Mars be the Billboard 200 #1 album for the week of March 28?
description:
Billboard updates the Billboard 200 albums chart each Tuesday (with adjusted release schedules on some holiday weeks), reflecting data from the previous week (Friday-Thursday). Each Billboard chart is then dated “Week of (date of the upcoming Saturday)”.

This market will resolve according to the number 1 album on the Billboard 200 chart dated “Week of March 28, 2026”.

This market will resolve as soon as the relevant chart is published. If the Billboard 200 chart for the specified week is not published within 14 calendar days of the expected release date, this market will resolve to “Other”.

The resolution source for this market will be the Billboard 200 chart for the specified week, published on the Billboard website (https://www.billboard.com/charts/billboard-200/) or through other official Billboard channels.

eventMetadata.context_description: Harry Styles' "Kiss All The Time. Disco, Occasionally." commands a commanding 99.3% implied probability for the Billboard 200 #1 spot dated March 29, driven by blockbuster first-week projections exceeding 350,000 equivalent album units from Hits Daily Double trackers, fueled by fervent fan pre-orders, vinyl bundles, and massive Spotify/Apple Music streams upon release. This eclipses rivals like Morgan Wallen's "I'm The Problem" (under 50k units) amid Wallen's ongoing chart strength but lacking comparable hype. Trader consensus reflects Styles' proven pop dominance, akin to his 521k debut for Harry's House. Realistic upsets hinge on anomalous late-week streaming surges or sales recount errors, though such reversals are exceedingly rare with this lead.

question: Will Neymar play in the 2026 FIFA World Cup?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if Neymar da Silva Santos Júnior takes the field as a player in at least one official match for Brazil during the 2026 FIFA World Cup at the group stage or later. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

Any on-field appearance as a player will qualify, in regulation, stoppage time, extra time, for a shootout, etc.

The primary resolution source for this market will be information from FIFA (https://www.fifa.com/), however a consensus of credible reporting will also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Neymar's chronic injury woes, including a 2023 ACL tear and subsequent thigh muscle tear sidelining him since February 2024, anchor trader consensus at 59.5% "No" for his 2026 World Cup participation. Now 32 and turning 34 by the tournament, his recovery has been slow—recently resuming Al-Hilal training in late September but yet to feature in competitive matches amid ongoing fitness concerns. Brazil's World Cup qualifiers proceed without him, with coach Dorival Júnior favoring emerging talents like Vinícius Júnior and Rodrygo, whose form reduces reliance on the veteran forward. While Neymar expresses intent to return, his limited recent playtime and age-related decline in explosiveness temper optimism reflected in market pricing.

question: Counter-Strike: fnatic vs aimclub (BO1) - BC Game Masters Championship Group A
description:
This market refers to the Counter-Strike match between fnatic and aimclub in the BC Game Masters Championship Group A, initially scheduled for March 24 at 6:00AM ET.

This market will resolve to "fnatic" if fnatic win the match against aimclub.

This market will resolve to "aimclub" if aimclub win the match against fnatic.

If the match is canceled (not played at all), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match begins but is not completed, and one team wins due to the opponent's forfeiture, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to the team who wins. 

If the match ends in a forfeit, disqualification, or walkover (team withdraws before the start and the other wins automatically), this market will resolve to 50-50. 

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://hltv.org. However, if https://hltv.org has not published final results within 2 hours after the event’s conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

eventMetadata.context_description: Fnatic enters as heavy favorites in this BO1 Group A clash at BC.Game Masters Championship, driven by their top-10 HLTV ranking and dominant recent form, including a 2-0 sweep over Eternal Fire last week. Aimclub, a rising Tier 2 squad, struggles with inconsistency, posting a 1-2 record in qualifiers and vulnerability on fnatic-preferred maps like Mirage and Inferno. Head-to-head favors fnatic 3-1 historically, with no major roster changes reported; however, BO1 volatility amplifies upset risk from aimclub's aggressive riflers. Traders price fnatic at ~75% implied probability, reflecting crowd wisdom on their experience edge amid group stage pressure for playoff seeding.

question: Map 1: Odd/Even Total Kills?
description:
This market will resolve to "Odd" if the total combined kills by both teams in Map 1 is an odd number.

This market will resolve to "Even" if the total combined kills by both teams in Map 1 is an even number.

"Total combined kills" includes all kills recorded during the match across all rounds, including kills during regulation rounds and overtime rounds if applicable. Team kills (friendly fire) and self-inflicted deaths (e.g., falling damage, own grenade) do not count as kills for the purposes of this market.

If no kills are recorded in Map 1, or if Map 1 is canceled (not played at all), or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Map 1 is never played due to forfeit, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Map 1 is not played because the series result has already been determined (e.g., a team clinches the series before Map 1 is needed), this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Map 1 is remade, resolution will be based on the remade game only.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from https://hltv.org. However, if https://hltv.org has not published final results within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

Head-to-head favors fnatic 3-1 historically, with no major roster changes reported; however, BO1 volatility amplifies upset risk from aimclub's aggressive riflers. Traders price fnatic at ~75% implied probability, reflecting crowd wisdom on their experience edge amid group stage pressure for playoff seeding.

question: Map 1: Odd/Even Total Rounds?
description:
This market will resolve to "Odd" if the total combined rounds played by both teams in Map 1 is an odd number.

This market will resolve to "Even" if the total combined rounds played by both teams in Map 1 is an even number.

"Total combined rounds" is the sum of rounds won by both teams during the match, including any overtime rounds if applicable. For example, a match ending 13–11 would have a total of 24 rounds (even), while a match ending 13–10 would have a total of 23 rounds (odd).

If Map 1 is canceled (not played at all) or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Map 1 is never played due to forfeit, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Map 1 is not played because the series result has already been determined (e.g., a team clinches the series before Map 1 is needed), this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Map 1 is remade, resolution will be based on the remade game only.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from https://hltv.org. However, if https://hltv.org has not published final results within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

eventMetadata.context_description: MOUZ NXT enters as clear trader favorites against Nexus in this pivotal Group A BO1 at the BC Game Masters Championship, reflecting their superior recent form with a 70% win rate over the last 10 maps versus Nexus's inconsistent 45%. The academy squad's young stars, including standout rifler Jimpphat, have dominated tier-2 events like CCT Europe, exploiting Nexus's vulnerabilities on Mirage and Inferno—likely map pool contenders. No major roster changes or bans reported, but MOUZ NXT's rest advantage after a lighter schedule bolsters momentum. Traders price in upset risk from BO1 volatility and Nexus's occasional clutch prowess, yet historical head-to-heads (2-0 MOUZ NXT) anchor the implied 65% probability. Schedule density post-match could influence rotations.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus in the BESTIA vs Tricked BO1 at BC.Game Masters Championship Group B tilts toward the Danish side, reflecting Tricked's stronger recent form with three straight series wins, including a 2-0 over Imperial, compared to BESTIA's mixed results capped by a 0-2 loss to Wildcard. BESTIA's aggressive Brazilian style thrives on upsets, yet Tricked's rest advantage post-bye and superior HLTV rankings underpin the implied probabilities, with Group B advancement on the line amid tight standings.

Team kills (friendly fire) and self-inflicted deaths (e.g., falling damage, own grenade) do not count as kills for the purposes of this market.

If no kills are recorded in Map 1, or if Map 1 is canceled (not played at all), or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Map 1 is never played due to forfeit, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Map 1 is not played because the series result has already been determined (e.g., a team clinches the series before Map 1 is needed), this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Map 1 is remade, resolution will be based on the remade game only.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from https://hltv.org. However, if https://hltv.org has not published final results within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

description:

question: Will "Age of Attraction" be the top global Netflix show this week?
description:
Netflix is expected to update its global Top 10 TV shows list on top10.netflix.com on Tuesday, March 24, 2026, 3:00 PM ET, reflecting viewership from the previous week (Monday to Sunday).

This market will resolve based on which show this update ranks as the #1 global Netflix show.

The ranking is based on total views globally, as reported by Netflix for TV shows (English only).

If the top10.netflix.com update does not occur by March 27, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "Other".

question: Will Iran successfully target shipping on March 20, 2026?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if Iranian forces conduct a kinetic strike on or otherwise seize control of a commercial ship on the specified date (IRST UTC +3:30). Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

Attacks on military vessels will not be considered.

Only military actions by Iranian forces explicitly claimed by the Islamic Republic of Iran, or confirmed to have originated from Iranian territory will count toward the resolution of this market. Attacks by proxy forces (i.e. Hezbollah, Houthis, etc.) will not count toward the resolution of this market.

Missile/drone strikes targeting a ship that are intercepted or otherwise do not directly impact the vessel will not be considered, regardless of damage through debris.

Qualifying incidents include, but are not limited to, drone and missile strikes, aerial bombings, and kinetic actions carried out by Iranian operatives in person, such as seizing a ship by force.

The primary resolution source for this market will be a consensus of credible reporting.

If the date/time of a qualifying incident cannot be confirmed by a consensus of credible reporting by the end of the second calendar date after the specified date, it will resolve to "No" regardless of whether a strike is later confirmed to have taken place.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket reflects low implied probability for Iran directly and successfully targeting international shipping, driven by deterrence from U.S. naval presence in the Strait of Hormuz and Red Sea amid Israel-Iran escalations. Recent developments include Iran's October 1 missile barrage on Israel, which prompted limited retaliation without shipping disruptions, while Iran-backed Houthis persist with Red Sea attacks on vessels linked to Israel—yet these are not classified as direct Iranian operations. Official Iranian statements emphasize restraint unless provoked further, prioritizing proxy actions. Upcoming catalysts include potential Israeli strikes on Iranian assets and UN Security Council sessions on regional tensions, which could shift risk assessments if rhetoric intensifies.

question: Will "Age of Attraction" be the #2 US Netflix show this week?
description:
Netflix is expected to update its Top 10 TV shows list on top10.netflix.com on Tuesday, March 24, 2026, 3:00 PM ET, reflecting viewership from the previous week (Monday to Sunday).

This market will resolve based on which show this update ranks as the #2 Netflix show in the United States.

The ranking is based on total views in the United States, as reported by Netflix for TV shows.

If the top10.netflix.com update does not occur by March 27, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "Other".

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus positions Virgin River Season 7 as the clear frontrunner at 41% implied probability for #2 on Netflix's US Top 10 TV chart this week, propelled by its explosive premiere fueled by rabid fan anticipation after Season 6's unresolved Mel pregnancy twist, amassing millions of hours viewed in days per Netflix's engagement reports. Unicorn Academy: Secrets Revealed trails at 17%, boosted by strong family streaming metrics in the kids animation niche amid school vacation buzz. One Piece Season 2's 7% reflects dedicated anime fandom hype from teaser drops, though it lags broader appeal. Recent Netflix weekly data shows holdover dominance from last week's #1 contenders, with social chatter amplifying Virgin River's viral word-of-mouth, while low-odds specials like Mark Normand's standup fade without crossover traction. Resolution hinges on Friday's official viewership hours update.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus positions Virgin River Season 7 as the clear frontrunner at 41% implied probability for #2 on Netflix's US Top 10 TV chart this week, propelled by its explosive premiere fueled by rabid fan anticipation after Season 6's unresolved Mel pregnancy twist, amassing millions of hours viewed in days per Netflix's engagement reports. Unicorn Academy: Secrets Revealed trails at 17%, boosted by strong family streaming metrics in the kids animation niche amid school vacation buzz. One Piece Season 2's 7% reflects dedicated anime fandom hype from teaser drops, though it lags broader appeal. Recent Netflix weekly data shows holdover dominance from last week's #1 contenders, with social chatter amplifying Virgin River's viral word-of-mouth, while low-odds specials like Mark Normand's standup fade without crossover traction. Resolution hinges on Friday's official viewership hours update.

question: Insurrection Act invoked by April 30?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if Donald Trump officially invokes the Insurrection Act of 1807 by April 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, it will resolve to "No".

For the purposes of this market, "invoke" means that the President formally announces the use of the Insurrection Act as legal authority for deploying active-duty U.S. military forces or federalizing the National Guard in response to civil disorder, insurrection, or rebellion.

The resolution source will be a consensus of credible reporting.

events.title: Insurrection Act invoked by...?
events.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if Donald Trump officially invokes the Insurrection Act of 1807 by the listed date, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, it will resolve to "No".

For the purposes of this market, "invoke" means that the President formally announces the use of the Insurrection Act as legal authority for deploying active-duty U.S. military forces or federalizing the National Guard in response to civil disorder, insurrection, or rebellion.

The resolution source will be a consensus of credible reporting.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket reflects low implied probabilities for invocation of the Insurrection Act by President Biden before January 20 or by incoming President Trump shortly after inauguration, driven by the law's rarity—last used in 1992 for Los Angeles riots—and legal hurdles requiring civil disorder threats unmet by current conditions. Recent catalysts include Trump's campaign pledges for mass deportations potentially testing military deployment limits, amid muted campus protests and no widespread insurrection signals. Historical precedents like 2020 unrest show threats rarely materialize without escalation. Key upcoming events: January 20 inauguration amid possible demonstrations, early executive orders on borders, and congressional oversight, any sparking disorder could shift odds higher.

question: Perle Labs FDV above $50M one day after launch?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if the Fully Diluted Valuation of Perle's governance token is greater than the value specified in the title 1 day after launch. Otherwise, the market will resolve to "No."

The token must be actively, publicly transferable and tradable to be considered a launch.

The FDV will be determined using the total token supply multiplied by the token price.

"1 day after launch" is defined as 4:00 PM ET on the calendar day following launch. The resolution source for this market is the most liquid price source available. If Perle Labs (https://x.com/PerleLabs) doesn't launch a token by December 31, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "No".

question: Will Tiger Woods play in the 2026 Masters Tournament?
description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if Tiger Woods participates in any of the four main rounds of the 2026 Masters Tournament played at Augusta National Golf Club. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

Only the four main Masters Tournament rounds will qualify for a “Yes” resolution. No practice rounds, Par 3 contest participation, honorary start or other play of any kind will be considered.

If the 2026 Masters Tournament is cancelled, or postponed after April 20, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to “No”.

The resolution source for this market will be the official leaderboard from the PGA and The Masters (https://www.masters.com/leaderboard); however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

events.title: Will Tiger Woods play in the 2026 Masters Tournament?
events.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if Tiger Woods participates in any of the four main rounds of the 2026 Masters Tournament played at Augusta National Golf Club. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

If the 2026 Masters Tournament is cancelled, or postponed after April 20, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to “No”.

eventMetadata.context_description: Tiger Woods' chronic back injuries and surgical history form the core driver behind traders' 64.5% implied probability favoring "No" for his 2026 Masters participation, underscoring doubts about his physical durability at age 50. Recent 2024 form reinforces this: Woods made just five PGA Tour starts, withdrawing midway at the PGA Championship due to illness atop nagging issues, missing cuts at the Genesis Invitational and U.S. Open, and struggling through a last-place finish at Augusta after barely advancing. While his lifetime exemption ensures eligibility, the tournament's walking demands and his limited practice rounds signal low odds of a full competitive commitment by April 2026 absent major health breakthroughs.

question: Will fewer than 5 ships be successfully targeted by Iran by March 31?
description:
This market will resolve based on the number of distinct commercial vessels on which Iranian forces conduct a kinetic strike or otherwise seize control between market creation and March 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET.

Attacks on military vessels will not be considered.

Only military actions by Iranian forces explicitly claimed by the Islamic Republic of Iran, or confirmed to have originated from Iranian territory, will count toward the resolution of this market. Attacks by proxy forces (i.e. Hezbollah, Houthis, etc.) will not count toward the resolution of this market.

Missile/drone strikes targeting a ship that are intercepted or otherwise do not directly impact the vessel will not be considered, regardless of damage through debris.

Qualifying incidents include, but are not limited to, drone and missile strikes, aerial bombings, and kinetic actions carried out by Iranian operatives in person, such as seizing a ship by force.

The primary resolution source for this market will be a consensus of credible reporting.

events.title: How many ships will Iran successfully target by March 31?
events.description:

Qualifying incidents include, but are not limited to, drone and missile strikes, aerial bombings, and kinetic actions carried out by Iranian operatives in person, such as seizing a ship by force.

The primary resolution source for this market will be a consensus of credible reporting.

If the date/time of a qualifying incident cannot be confirmed by a consensus of credible reporting by the end of the second calendar date after the specified date, it will not count, regardless of whether a strike is later confirmed to have taken place.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket heavily favors fewer than five ships successfully targeted by Iran by March 31, with 92% implied probability reflecting real capital backing de-escalation signals amid robust U.S.-led naval intercepts in the Red Sea. Recent Houthi attacks—Iran's primary proxy—have yielded minimal confirmed successes, with over 90% of drones and missiles downed by coalition forces, stabilizing shipping insurance premiums and Brent crude futures around $85/barrel. Heightened U.S. carrier deployments and diplomatic overtures from Tehran further anchor low escalation odds. A realistic challenge would require direct Iranian naval strikes or Houthi breakthroughs past defenses, potentially spiking global freight rates if tensions reignite before quarter-end.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket heavily favors fewer than five ships successfully targeted by Iran by March 31, with 92% implied probability reflecting real capital backing de-escalation signals amid robust U.S.-led naval intercepts in the Red Sea. Recent Houthi attacks—Iran's primary proxy—have yielded minimal confirmed successes, with over 90% of drones and missiles downed by coalition forces, stabilizing shipping insurance premiums and Brent crude futures around $85/barrel. Heightened U.S. carrier deployments and diplomatic overtures from Tehran further anchor low escalation odds. A realistic challenge would require direct Iranian naval strikes or Houthi breakthroughs past defenses, potentially spiking global freight rates if tensions reignite before quarter-end.

question: Will 20 or more ships be successfully targeted by Iran by March 31?
description:
This market will resolve based on the number of distinct commercial vessels on which Iranian forces conduct a kinetic strike or otherwise seize control between market creation and March 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET.

Attacks on military vessels will not be considered.

Only military actions by Iranian forces explicitly claimed by the Islamic Republic of Iran, or confirmed to have originated from Iranian territory, will count toward the resolution of this market. Attacks by proxy forces (i.e. Hezbollah, Houthis, etc.) will not count toward the resolution of this market.

Missile/drone strikes targeting a ship that are intercepted or otherwise do not directly impact the vessel will not be considered, regardless of damage through debris.

Qualifying incidents include, but are not limited to, drone and missile strikes, aerial bombings, and kinetic actions carried out by Iranian operatives in person, such as seizing a ship by force.

The primary resolution source for this market will be a consensus of credible reporting.

question: Will Hikaru Nakamura win the 2026 FIDE Candidates Tournament?
description:
This market will resolve to the player that wins the 2026 FIDE Candidates Tournament scheduled for March 29 - April 16, 2026.

If at any point it becomes impossible for a listed player to win the 2026 FIDE Candidates Tournament per the rules of the FIDE, the corresponding market will resolve to “No”.

If the 2026 FIDE Candidates Tournament is cancelled, or postponed after April 30, 2026, or there is otherwise no winner declared within that timeframe, this market will resolve to “Other”.

The primary resolution source will be official information from FIDE; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Fabiano Caruana leads trader consensus at 29.5% implied probability for the 2026 FIDE Candidates due to his world No. 2 classical rating above 2800 and proven track record as a multiple-time challenger with deep tournament experience. Hikaru Nakamura trails at 19.5%, bolstered by consistent top finishes and rapid/blitz dominance that translates to Candidates pressure, though his classical edge lags Caruana's. Rising prodigy Javokhir Sindarov (16%) and Praggnanandhaa Rameshbabu (13%) gain traction from recent breakout performances, including Sindarov's FIDE Circuit surge and Pragg's Olympiad contributions, signaling upside in an open field where youth momentum challenges veterans like Anish Giri and Wei Yi. Odds reflect qualification uncertainties amid the 2025 cycle.

question: SEC removes quarterly reporting requirement?
description:
The U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC) may be preparing a proposal that could eliminate the requirement for publicly traded companies to file quarterly earnings reports. You can read more about that here: https://www.reuters.com/business/finance/us-sec-preparing-eliminate-quarterly-reporting-requirement-wsj-says-2026-03-16/.

This market will resolve to "Yes" if the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission votes to approve a rule or otherwise formally enacts a policy that removes the requirement for publicly traded companies to file quarterly earnings reports by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

Narrow company or industry specific removals of quarterly earnings requirements will not qualify. Likewise a general removal of the rules which maintains the quarterly reporting requirement for specific companies will qualify.

Any approving vote on a rule change that reduces the requirement to report earnings from quarterly to a less frequent cadence will qualify.

The primary resolution source will be official information from the SEC; however, a consensus of credible reporting will also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader sentiment on the SEC eliminating quarterly reporting requirements tilts toward "No" at 61.5% market-implied probability, driven primarily by regulatory inertia and the agency's longstanding emphasis on frequent disclosures for investor protection, with no formal proposal or rulemaking underway. Corporate leaders like Elon Musk have advocated for semi-annual reporting to curb short-termism, echoing sentiments from 2023 Business Roundtable letters, yet SEC Chair Gary Gensler's recent statements prioritize transparency amid market volatility. Absent a catalyst like a Commission vote or legislative push—next FOMC-adjacent meetings unlikely to shift focus—traders see entrenched 10-Q mandates persisting, pricing in low odds of near-term change against historical resistance to easing Sarbanes-Oxley standards.

question: Will Israel conduct military action in Greater Beirut on March 19, 2026?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if Israel initiates a drone, missile, or air strike on Greater Beirut on the listed date Israel Standard Time (IST). Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

For the purposes of this market, a qualifying "strike" is defined as the use of aerial bombs, drones, or missiles (including cruise or ballistic missiles) launched by Israeli military forces that impact within Greater Beirut.

For the purposes of this market, “Greater Beirut” refers to the continuous urbanized zone that encompasses all of the Beirut Governorate and the adjacent coastal and suburban municipalities of the Mount Lebanon Governorate. For this market, the geographic boundaries as defined in the map “Location of the Greater Beirut Area” (https://polymarket-upload.s3.us-east-2.amazonaws.com/Beirut-3ffb23044f.png) from Faour & Mhawej (2014), Mapping Urban Transitions in the Greater Beirut Area Using Different Space Platforms (https://www.mdpi.com/2073-445X/3/3/941) will be used. If the precise location of a strike cannot be clearly attributed to the defined territory based on the referenced map, it will not qualify.

Missiles or drones that are intercepted and surface-to-air missile strikes will not be sufficient for a "Yes" resolution, regardless of whether they land on the specified territory or cause damage.

Actions such as artillery fire, small arms fire, FPV or ground based ATGM strikes, ground incursions, naval shelling, cyberattacks, or other operations conducted by Israeli ground operatives will not qualify.

The primary resolution source will be official government/military statements (Israeli or foreign), multilateral bodies (UN, etc.), or a consensus of credible reporting from major international media and national broadcasters/newspapers.

If the date/time of a strike cannot be confirmed by a consensus of credible reporting by the end of the third calendar date after this market's end date, it will resolve to "No" regardless of whether a strike was later confirmed to have taken place.

eventMetadata.context_description: A US-brokered ceasefire between Israel and Hezbollah, effective November 27, 2024, has sharply lowered trader consensus on imminent Israeli military action against Beirut, with markets pricing low probabilities amid de-escalation signals like Israeli troop withdrawals from southern Lebanon and halted cross-border fire. Key drivers include Hezbollah's diminished command structure after Israel's September strikes killing leader Hassan Nasrallah in Beirut's suburbs, alongside diplomatic pressure from the US and France. Recent minor violations persist, but no major Beirut operations reported since late November. Traders eye UNIFIL monitoring and potential Netanyahu government statements, with any verified Hezbollah rocket launches or terror plots risking renewed airstrikes.
